Raneem Sadaky

My Story

From Syria to Brussels:
Growth & Purpose

I am someone whose life has been shaped by movement, responsibility, and purpose. Growing up between Syria and Brussels required early adaptation and a steady sense of direction — giving me something a classroom alone cannot: the ability to navigate complexity with maturity.

Today I am a Belgian-Syrian MBA student at KU Leuven. My internship at the Syrian Embassy in Brussels confirmed that international, high-responsibility environments are where I belong — drafting policy summaries, supporting institutional correspondence, and contributing to the Embassy's public presence.

Unique Value Proposition

Academic Research

Master's Thesis

MBA Thesis — KU Leuven, 2025–2026
True Pricing and Consumer Behaviour
"How does revealing hidden environmental and social costs of products influence purchase intention, and do these effects vary by product type and consumers' environmental concern?"

An empirical study analysing behavioural responses to price transparency interventions. The research examines interaction effects between product characteristics and individual-level environmental attitudes, using quantitative methods to assess causal relationships — work with direct relevance to international economic governance and sustainability policy.

Vision

Where I Am Going

Near-Term Direction
Working in international and political environments where communication and cross-cultural understanding shape outcomes — EU institutions, embassies, international organizations, and Syrian governmental bodies. Spaces where I can contribute a voice shaped by two worlds.
Long-Term Vision
Leading my own consulting firm, helping organizations build strategy, communication, and sustainable development initiatives — advising, guiding, and connecting cultures and ideas. My path combines public service and entrepreneurship because my identity combines both heart and action.
"To connect people and ideas in a way that empowers communities and builds hope — one conversation, one project, one bridge at a time."
